name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across North America and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn shop operator with over 3,300 retail locations. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need access to quick liquidity without traditional credit requirements. FirstCash operates stores across 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador, employing approximately 22,000 people across all regions.

The company's core business model centers on pawn loans secured by pledged personal property—a non-recourse lending product where customers receive cash loans against items they own. FirstCash buys and sells a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Beyond pawn lending, FirstCash offers layaway services (10% down payment plans), gold and precious metals buying, and retail merchandise sales. Through its wholly owned subsidiary AFF, the company also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale—over 3,300 locations make it the dominant international pawn operator—and geographic diversification across multiple countries. The company is a component of both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating significant institutional recognition. Their technology-driven point-of-sale systems and integrated payment solutions set them apart from smaller, independent pawn shops. The breadth of merchandise they buy and sell creates a genuine retail marketplace alongside the lending function.

For consumers, FirstCash pawn loans offer immediate cash without credit checks or income verification, but carry the risk of losing pledged items if loans aren't repaid. Interest rates and terms aren't disclosed on their website, making comparison difficult. The service is best suited for short-term cash needs where the customer has valuable collateral they can afford to lose. Like all pawn operations, this is fundamentally a high-cost lending alternative appropriate only when traditional credit is unavailable.
pros: Massive 3,300+ store network across 29 U.S. states and international locations, ensuring accessibility
Non-recourse pawn loans require no credit check, income verification, or employment history
Instant cash offers—customers get paid on the spot for items purchased or pawned
Accepts diverse collateral types including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, and musical instruments
No repayment obligation; if unable to reclaim item, no credit damage or debt collection occurs
Layaway service with affordable 10% down payment option for retail purchases
Technology-driven point-of-sale systems and integrated payment solutions through AFF subsidiary
Listed on major indices (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) indicating regulatory compliance and stability
cons: Website discloses no specific interest rates, loan terms, or fees, making cost comparison impossible before visiting a store
Risk of losing valuable personal property permanently if unable to repay the pawn loan
Pawn loans are a high-cost alternative to traditional credit with implied triple-digit APRs typical in the industry
Limited product detail—no explanation of loan duration, grace periods, or renewal options on website
International and multi-state operation creates potential inconsistency in loan terms across locations
best_for: Consumers with valuable collateral and immediate cash needs who lack access to traditional credit
Those seeking short-term liquidity without credit checks or impact on credit scores
Individuals buying merchandise who prefer layaway over upfront payment or credit cards
Sellers of gold, jewelry, or electronics seeking quick cash without consignment wait times
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers who own valuable items and need immediate cash but lack access to traditional credit products. The critical caveat is that pawn loans carry inherent risk of asset loss and represent a high-cost borrowing option; this service should only be used when the borrower can afford losing the pledged item and has exhausted lower-cost alternatives.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property pledges
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Gold, silver, and precious metals buying at spot rates with cash payment
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Used merchandise retail sales from store inventory
Lease-to-own financing through AFF subsidiary network (15,000+ merchant partners)
Retail payment solutions and point-of-sale technology services
Store locator and online inventory search tools
Consumer merchandise buying—accepts 'almost anything of value'
